title: Prolongoa pectinata
taxonomy: Prolongoa pectinata is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It is native to Madagascar and is classified as a member of the genus Prolongoa. Further classification of Prolongoa pectinata is within the tribe Rupestres. Within this tribe, Prolongoa pectinata is part of the subfamily Acanthoideae. This classification is based on botanical studies and classification systems.

intro: Prolongoa pectinata, also known as "Malabar nut," is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, malaria, and other health conditions, due to its ability to induce sweating and relieve body aches. The bioactive constituents of Prolongoa pectinata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Prolongoa pectinata include teas, infusions, and extracts, often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
